articles.title: Scientists Create Material Stronger Than Diamond
articles.summary: A team of chemists made a new form of carbon called 'amorphous diamond' that is harder than regular diamonds. It could be used to make ultra-tough coatings for tools and electronics.

articles.title: New 'Living Plastic' Breaks Itself Down After Use
articles.summary: Chemists embedded bacteria spores inside a new type of plastic that wake up and eat the plastic when it ends up in the environment — potentially solving the plastic pollution crisis.

articles.title: Scientists Turn Seawater Into Drinking Water With Just Sunlight
articles.summary: A new chemical filter made from tiny salt-capturing molecules can desalinate seawater using only the sun's heat, with no electricity required — a breakthrough for water-scarce regions.

articles.title: Glowing Molecule Found That Can Kill Cancer Cells With Light
articles.summary: Researchers developed a molecule that enters cancer cells and, when activated by light, produces a burst of energy that destroys the cell from the inside without harming healthy tissue.

articles.title: Chemistry Students Win Prize for Turning Banana Peels Into Fuel
articles.summary: A group of high-school students discovered a chemical process to convert banana peel waste into clean-burning hydrogen gas — which could one day help power cars and homes.

articles.title: James Webb Space Telescope Spots Ancient Galaxies From the Dawn of Time
articles.summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has captured images of galaxies that formed just 300 million years after the Big Bang, helping scientists understand how the early universe looked.

articles.title: Astronauts Complete Spacewalk to Upgrade Space Station Solar Panels
articles.summary: Two astronauts spent over six hours outside the International Space Station installing new solar panels that will boost its power supply for future missions.

articles.title: Scientists Find Evidence of Ancient Water on Mars
articles.summary: New data from NASA's Perseverance rover suggests that a large lake once filled Jezero Crater on Mars billions of years ago, raising hopes for finding signs of ancient life.

articles.title: Massive Solar Storm Creates Spectacular Auroras Around the World
articles.summary: A powerful burst of energy from the Sun sent particles streaming to Earth, creating dazzling northern lights visible as far south as Texas and southern Europe.

articles.title: New Exoplanet Found in the 'Goldilocks Zone' of a Nearby Star
articles.summary: Astronomers have discovered a planet orbiting a star just 40 light-years away that sits in the habitable zone — not too hot, not too cold — where liquid water could exist.

articles.title: Scientists Discover a New Species of Glowing Sea Turtle
articles.summary: Researchers filming the ocean floor at night were astonished to find a previously unknown sea turtle that glows red and green — making it the first known biofluorescent reptile.

articles.title: Elephants Use Names to Call Each Other, Study Finds
articles.summary: Scientists recorded elephant calls in Kenya and discovered that elephants address each other with unique sounds that act like names — a rare ability found mainly in humans and dolphins.

articles.title: Record Number of Sea Turtles Nest on Florida Beaches
articles.summary: Conservation efforts have paid off: Florida beaches recorded the highest number of loggerhead sea turtle nests in 40 years, a hopeful sign that the endangered species is recovering.

articles.title: Honeybees Learned to Open Bottles by Watching Other Bees
articles.summary: In a remarkable experiment, honeybees watched other bees unscrew bottle caps to get to sugar water inside — and then did it themselves, showing bees can learn by observation.

articles.title: Rare Pink Manta Ray Spotted for Only the Second Time Ever
articles.summary: A pink manta ray nicknamed 'Inspector Clouseau' was photographed off Australia's Great Barrier Reef. Scientists believe a genetic condition changes its pigment from white to bright pink.

articles.title: Ocean Temperatures Hit Record High for Third Year in a Row
articles.summary: Scientists report that the world's oceans are warmer than ever recorded, which is causing more intense storms, bleaching coral reefs, and disrupting sea creatures' habitats.

articles.title: New Volcano Discovered Hiding Under a Mile of Antarctic Ice
articles.summary: Scientists using radar discovered a huge volcano buried deep beneath Antarctica's ice sheet. They say it has been erupting slowly for thousands of years, melting ice from underneath.

articles.title: World's Largest Reef System Shows Signs of Recovery
articles.summary: Sections of Australia's Great Barrier Reef recorded their highest coral cover in decades after cooler ocean temperatures gave corals a chance to regrow from recent bleaching events.

articles.title: Mysterious Methane Clouds Spotted Above Arctic Tundra
articles.summary: Satellite images show unusually large methane clouds rising from melting permafrost in Siberia — a sign that frozen ground is thawing faster than expected as temperatures rise.

articles.title: Scientists Plant Trees Using Drone Seed Bombs in Deforested Areas
articles.summary: A team of engineers has developed drones that shoot seed pods into the ground at speeds of 120 miles per hour, planting up to 40,000 trees per day in hard-to-reach areas.

articles.title: Robot Dog Learns to Walk on Its Own Just Like a Puppy
articles.summary: Researchers taught a four-legged robot to walk by trial and error in just a few hours — the same way real animals learn — without any human programming of each movement.

articles.title: New Battery Charges a Phone Completely in Under Two Minutes
articles.summary: Scientists announced a new battery technology using tiny carbon tubes that stores electricity much faster than today's lithium batteries and lasts thousands more charge cycles.

articles.title: Scientists Build Contact Lens That Can Zoom In With a Blink
articles.summary: Engineers created a soft electronic contact lens that lets the wearer zoom in on distant objects simply by blinking twice — it tracks tiny eye movements to control the zoom.

articles.title: Solar-Powered Plane Completes Longest Unmanned Flight Ever
articles.summary: A solar-powered drone stayed airborne for 64 days straight, powered only by sunlight, breaking the world record and paving the way for permanent high-altitude communications platforms.

articles.title: 3D Printers Can Now Build Entire Concrete Houses in 24 Hours
articles.summary: Construction robots using giant 3D printers can now build the concrete walls of a full-sized house in one day, which could help provide affordable homes much faster than traditional building.

articles.title: Scientists Achieve Nuclear Fusion With More Energy Out Than In
articles.summary: For the first time, a fusion experiment produced more energy than the lasers used to trigger it — a milestone in the quest to harness the same power that makes stars shine as a clean energy source.

articles.title: New Superconductor Works at Room Temperature, Scientists Claim
articles.summary: Physicists announced a material that conducts electricity with zero resistance at room temperature, which could one day make power lines perfectly efficient and levitating trains possible.

articles.title: CERN Detects a Brand-New Subatomic Particle
articles.summary: Scientists at the world's biggest particle accelerator have spotted a new particle that lasts for less than a billionth of a second — giving physicists fresh clues about what matter is made of.

articles.title: Physicists Build a Clock So Precise It Won't Lose a Second in 300 Billion Years
articles.summary: A new atomic clock using ytterbium atoms is so accurate it could detect tiny differences in the flow of time caused by gravity — confirming Einstein's theory in real time.

articles.title: Scientists Slow Light Down to the Speed of a Bicycle
articles.summary: Researchers fired laser light through an ultra-cold gas and slowed it from 300 million metres per second to just 17 metres per second — slower than a car on a motorway.
